title: poa nemoralis f. firmula
intro: Poanemoralis f. firmula, also known as the wood meadow-grass, is a subspecies of the Poa nemoralis grass, a type of perennial grass native to Europe and western Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help to soothe wounds and reduce swelling. The health benefits of Poa nemoralis f. firmula include its ability to promote wound healing, reduce fever, and ease digestive issues. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
